reasoning:
Our search confirms that as of November 2025, Bobby Finke is the official world record holder in the men's long course 1500m freestyle with a time of 14:30.67, set at the Paris 2024 Olympics. The 2025 Long Course World Championships have already taken place in July/August 2025 in Singapore, and Finke's record survived. 

Historically, long course 1500m world records are rarely broken outside of peak summer international competitions (Olympics or LC World Championships). The previous 1500m world record set by Sun Yang stood for 12 years. Furthermore, the timeframe between November 2025 and February 2026 coincides with the short-course (25m) swimming season (with Short Course World Championships typically in December) and heavy winter training phases for distance swimmers, making a long-course world record attempt almost vanishingly unlikely. Lastly, if Finke manages to lower his own record during this time, he would still be the holder, which resolves YES.

Given the exceedingly low base rate of 1500m LC records being broken in the winter season, no major upcoming LC international championships between November and February, and the fact that a retrospective doping disqualification is extremely rare, the event is incredibly likely to resolve YES. Probabilities are capped at 0.95 according to the absolute maximum forecasting bounds, and slightly decay towards 0.90 for the 3-month horizon to indicate increasing uncertainty over time as required.
